name: Anker Soundcore Q45 Adaptive Active Noise Cancelling Headphones

specs.ANC: Adaptive ANC with 3 modes (Transport, Indoor, Outdoor)
specs.Special Features: Hi-Res Audio certified, foldable, app EQ

whatWorks: LDAC Hi-Res Audio support at $79 CAD is extraordinary value — a codec typically reserved for headphones at 3-4× the price delivers noticeably better wireless audio quality on compatible Android devices.
50h battery (ANC off) rivals headphones costing $300+ more. For budget-conscious buyers who hate charging anxiety, the Q45 is essentially set-and-forget for a full workweek.

Three adaptive ANC modes tune noise cancellation for your environment — Transport mode for transit and planes, Indoor for office use, and Outdoor for open spaces. The adaptation is genuine and effective for the price tier.
Multipoint Bluetooth allows simultaneous connection to two devices — laptop and phone work together without re-pairing, a feature that often costs $200+ on competing headphones.
worthKnowing: ANC performance doesn't approach Sony WH-1000XM6 or Bose QuietComfort 45 — at $79 vs $350+, this is expected, but buyers comparing ANC quality will notice the gap in loud transit environments.
LDAC requires a compatible Android device and a LDAC-capable source — iPhone users and many Android users without LDAC support won't benefit from the Hi-Res certification.
The earcup memory foam compresses noticeably after 2-3 hours of continuous wear. Long listening sessions can cause discomfort around the ears that premium headphones with deeper cups avoid.

Build quality reflects the price — the plastic chassis is lightweight but doesn't feel premium. Travel stress and drops may cause issues over time that more expensive aluminum-framed competitors resist.

commonComplaints.title: ANC is fine for the price…
commonComplaints.body: ANC is fine for the price but don't compare it to Sony or Bose — it cuts background noise but doesn't eliminate it.

commonComplaints.title: Earcups get uncomfortable after about two…
commonComplaints.body: Earcups get uncomfortable after about two hours — the foam isn't deep enough for my ears and they press against the driver.

commonComplaints.title: LDAC only on Android which is…
commonComplaints.body: LDAC only on Android which is a big caveat — iPhone users just get AAC and miss out on the main audio selling point.

commonComplaints.title: Plastic build makes me nervous about…
commonComplaints.body: Plastic build makes me nervous about long-term durability — feels fine right now but I wouldn't trust it with rough treatment.

verdict: The Anker Soundcore Q45 punches far above its $79 CAD price by offering LDAC Hi-Res Audio, 50h battery, three-mode adaptive ANC, and multipoint connectivity — a feature set that costs $300+ on premium competitors. ANC won't satisfy Sony or Bose expectations, and earcup comfort falls off after long sessions. For budget-conscious buyers, students, or anyone wanting capable everyday headphones without spending $300, the Q45 is the clear value leader in the over-ear category.

realBuyers.quote: LDAC at $79 is insane — I A/B tested against my friend's Sony XM5 and the difference is smaller than I expected.
50 hours battery. I charge these once a week. It's not even a thought anymore.
ANC on the subway takes the edge off — not silent, but enough to focus on podcasts without having to turn up the volume.
Best headphones you can buy for under $100 — it's not even close. Multipoint, LDAC, 50h battery. Ridiculous value.

sources.title: Anker Soundcore Q45 Review — Android Authority

metaDescription: Anker Soundcore Q45 review for Canada: LDAC Hi-Res Audio, 50h battery, adaptive ANC, multipoint at $79. Honest on ANC limits and comfort. ~$79 CAD.
introText: The Anker Soundcore Q45 are budget over-ear headphones that punch well above their price — offering LDAC Hi-Res Audio, 50h battery, three-mode adaptive ANC, and multipoint Bluetooth at $79 CAD. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B09T12M7GN) at around $79 CAD.
